text:
Artificial intelligence is changing how people work. Many routine tasks, like data entry and scheduling, are now automated, freeing employees for more creative work. In offices, AI-powered productivity tools help with writing emails, tracking projects, and analyzing information quickly. This saves time and reduces human error.

Rather than replacing jobs entirely, AI often works alongside people. For example, customer service agents now use chatbots to answer common questions while they handle complex problems. This human-AI collaboration boosts efficiency and job satisfaction.

However, some roles are disappearing, while new ones are emerging. Workers need to learn new skills, such as how to use AI tools and interpret their results. Adaptability is key in this changing workplace.
